# be-semantic

> A domain-first design methodology for building applications from meaning, not from technical constraints. The workflow: Story → ALPS → Fake → Agreement → Schema → Be Implementation.

## Core Concepts

- Design starts from user intent (stories), not from technology (endpoints, tables)
- ALPS defines application vocabulary and state transitions, independent of implementation
- 50 realistic fake data entries reveal domain constraints through observation
- Schemas record observed reality; they are not technical prescriptions
- Be Framework implements the domain as states of being (Being/Final), not actions

## Workflow

The six steps in order:
1. **Story** — Write user stories ("As a user, I want X, because Y"). The "because" is mandatory; it reveals domain intent.
2. **ALPS** — Define application vocabulary (semantic descriptors) and state transitions (safe/unsafe/idempotent). Validate with `asd`.
3. **Fake** — Generate 50+ realistic fake data entries using an AI model. Make them look like real user data, not test data.
4. **Agreement** — Review fake data with stakeholders. Discover constraints through observation, not prescription.
5. **Schema** — Write JSON Schema based on observations (`maxLength: 80` because the observed max was 41, not because VARCHAR defaults to 255).
6. **Be** — Implement as Being (states) and Final (transitions) in Be Framework. Schema required fields become constructor arguments.

## Key Principle

> Constraints should be derived from observed reality, not prescribed from abstract rules.

When helping users apply this methodology:
- Always start with stories before any technical artifact
- The "because" clause in stories is not optional
- Fake data must feel realistic, not randomly generated
- Schema maxLength/minLength values should be derived from fake data observations
- ALPS descriptor IDs should be used as property names in schemas and Being classes for consistency

## Documentation

- [Tutorial (ja)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/ja/tutorial.md): Step-by-step guide from zero to working Be app, including tool installation
- [Tutorial (en)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/en/tutorial.md): Step-by-step guide from zero to working Be app, including tool installation
- [Concept (en)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/en/concept.md): Philosophy and motivation — why domain precedes pattern
- [Concept (ja)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/ja/concept.md): Philosophy and motivation in Japanese
- [Workflow (en)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/en/workflow.md): Detailed step-by-step guide with checkpoints
- [Workflow (ja)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/ja/workflow.md): Detailed step-by-step guide in Japanese
- [Insights (en)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/en/insights.md): Observations and reflections on the methodology
- [Insights (ja)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/docs/ja/insights.md): Observations and reflections in Japanese

## Example Files

- [Story example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/example/story/main.md): Complete user stories for a Todo app
- [ALPS example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/example/alps/todo.json): Complete ALPS profile for a Todo app
- [Fake data example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/example/fake/data-50.json): 50 realistic fake Todo entries
- [Schema: TodoList](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/example/schema/todo-list.json): JSON Schema derived from fake data observations
- [Schema: TodoDetail](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/koriym/be-semantic/main/example/schema/todo-detail.json): JSON Schema with observation comments

## Related Tools

- [Be Framework](https://be-framework.github.io/): The ontological PHP framework ("Be, Don't Do")
- [ALPS](https://alps.io/): Application-Level Profile Semantics specification
- [app-state-diagram (asd)](https://www.app-state-diagram.com/): Visualize ALPS profiles as state diagrams. Install: `brew install alps-asd/asd/asd`
- [Semantic-Ex Method](https://koriym.github.io/blog/2025/08/10/semantic-method-en): The original article describing the fake-data observation technique

## Optional

- [Be Framework full docs](https://be-framework.github.io/llms-full.txt): Complete Be Framework documentation for LLMs — Being, Final, Metamorphosis, Diamond Pattern, and all concepts
- [app-state-diagram full docs](https://www.app-state-diagram.com/llms-full.txt): Complete ALPS and app-state-diagram documentation for LLMs — profile format, descriptors, state transitions, and asd CLI usage
